content.text: Workspace repo pushed fine. The `.openclaw` config repo hit GitHub push protection — a session trajectory file contains a leaked PAT. I need to strip that secret from the commit history before pushing. Let me handle this carefully.

arguments.command: cd /Users/landonking/.openclaw && grep -n 'ghp_REDACTED\|gho_\|github_pat_' agents/main/sessions/46952611-434e-4636-ac4d-a8735da71f5f.trajectory.jsonl 2>/dev/null | head -5

The `.openclaw` config repo has session logs with a leaked PAT in the trajectory files. Same issue the previous run hit. I need to scrub the token, amend the commit, and force-push.

arguments.command:
cd /Users/landonking/.openclaw && \
# Broad scrub: replace any ghp_REDACTED tokens (36+ char suffixes) across all files
find agents/ logs/ -type f \( -name '*.jsonl' -o -name '*.json' \) 2>/dev/null | while read f; do
  if grep -qP 'ghp_REDACTED[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
    perl -i -pe 's/ghp_REDACTED[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}/ghp_REDACTED[REDACTED]/g' "$f"
    echo "Scrubbed: $f"
  fi
done

arguments.command:

# Scrub ALL ghp_REDACTED fragments from all jsonl/json files in the repo
find . -type f \( -name '*.jsonl' -o -name '*.json' -o -name '*.md' \) ! -path './.git/*' 2>/dev/null | while read f; do
  if grep -q 'ghp_REDACTED' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -i '' 's/ghp_REDACTED[A-Za-z0-9_]*/ghp_REDACTED/g' "$f"
    echo "Scrubbed: $f"

done
